skill.name: move-code-quality
skill.description: Reviews Move packages against the Move Book Code Quality Checklist, with emphasis on Move 2024 Edition style, manifest settings, APIs, tests, and comments.
skill.summary: Reviews Move code for Move 2024 Edition quality and style issues.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged dynamic execution, weak cryptography, external commands, reconnaissance, and a hardcoded URL. Manual review found no executable scripts, command execution, cryptography, data collection, or prompt injection; the matches are Markdown code examples, checklist text, and one public Move Book reference.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Matches Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The external command, dynamic function constructor, and weak cryptography alerts point to Move syntax examples and checklist prose. I found no evidence of executable JavaScript, shell commands, Ruby backticks, cryptographic implementation, or malicious intent.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is a Markdown skill guide, and the flagged lines are inline examples or headings. No executable file or instruction asks the agent to run these snippets as host commands.

low_findings.title: Documentation Link Is Benign
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL is a reference to the Move Book Code Quality Checklist. I found no evidence of network requests, credential submission, tracking endpoints, or data exfiltration behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears only in the references section and is not embedded in executable code. It supports the stated purpose of the skill.

content.value_statement: Move projects can drift from current Move 2024 Edition conventions. This skill checks packages against the Move Book checklist and gives prioritized review feedback.
actual_capabilities: Reviews Move.toml files for edition settings, framework dependencies, and named address conventions.
Checks Move modules for modern syntax, imports, constants, structs, and function signatures.
Identifies style improvements for method chaining, option macros, loop macros, and collection access.
Reviews Move tests for attributes, naming, assertions, cleanup, and TestScenario usage.
Produces prioritized findings with file paths, line numbers, impact, and recommended fixes.
limitations: Does not compile, execute, or formally verify Move code by itself.
Depends on readable project files and accurate line numbers in the active workspace.
Focuses on quality checklist compliance, not full economic or protocol security review.
Recommendations assume Move 2024 Edition and recent Sui framework conventions.

use_cases.title: Package Quality Review
use_cases.description: Review a Move package before merge and identify checklist issues that should be fixed first.

use_cases.title: Manifest Modernization
use_cases.description: Check Move.toml for current edition settings, dependency style, and named address hygiene.

use_cases.title: Test Suite Cleanup
use_cases.description: Find outdated Move test patterns and replace them with clearer Move 2024 Edition conventions.

prompt_templates.title: Review This Package
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Move package for code quality issues. Prioritize Move 2024 Edition problems and list the highest impact fixes first.

prompt_templates.title: Check Manifest Quality
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Move.toml file for edition, dependency, and named address issues. Explain each recommendation in simple terms.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect Function Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these Move modules for function visibility, parameter ordering, composability, getters, and modern method syntax.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Tests and Macros
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this package for test quality, option macros, loop macros, collection syntax, and comment quality. Group findings by severity.

output_examples.input: Review a Move package that lacks an edition setting and uses older module syntax.
output: Summary of passed checks and high priority issues.
A critical finding for the missing Move 2024 Edition setting.
An important improvement recommending modern module declaration syntax.
Clear next steps ordered by impact.

output_examples.input: Review a Move test module with prefixed test names and basic equality assertions.
output: Test naming feedback that removes redundant prefixes.
Assertion guidance that prefers clearer equality checks.
Notes about avoiding unnecessary cleanup in expected failure tests.

best_practices: Run the review from the package root so Move.toml and source files are both visible.
Ask for a focused category review when you only need manifest, tests, or function design feedback.
Apply critical edition and dependency fixes before lower priority formatting improvements.
anti_patterns: Do not treat this skill as a replacement for compiler checks or formal verification.
Do not ask it to rewrite large packages without first reviewing the prioritized findings.
Do not apply Move 2024 Edition recommendations to projects that intentionally target older editions.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews Move packages against the Move Book Code Quality Checklist, including manifests, modules, functions, tests, and comments.

faq.question: Does it execute Move code?
faq.answer: No. It reads project files and gives review feedback. Use your normal Move tooling to compile and test changes.

faq.question: Can it fix issues automatically?
faq.answer: It can suggest specific edits and may help apply them when your coding tool has file editing access.

faq.question: Is this a security audit skill?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on code quality and style. Smart contract security review still requires dedicated analysis.

faq.question: Which Move version does it assume?
faq.answer: It assumes Move 2024 Edition and recent Sui framework conventions, including Sui 1.45 or later dependency guidance.

faq.question: Can it review one file only?
faq.answer: Yes. Ask for a specific file or category review when you do not need a full package scan.
